_meta.description: Maps IESP control domains to Tech-Audit/IESP QA procedures. The Tech-Audit/IESP domain audits the quality of IESP assessments — it does not perform the IESP assessment itself.

domainMappings.rationale: Access control is a Part D (Cybersecurity) domain — QA procedures verify whether the IESP assessed this domain with adequate scope (SD-01, SD-02), methodology (MC-01), evidence (FQ-01, FQ-02), and reporting (RQ-01). Engagement acceptance procedures (EA-01 to EA-03) apply universally.
Physical security assessment quality is verified through the same QA procedures — scoping (SD), methodology (MC), fieldwork (FQ), and reporting (RQ).
Operations security covers multiple RMiT clauses (10.4-10.45) — risk-based scoping (SD-03) and approach selection (MC-04) are critical for ensuring the IESP prioritised the most relevant sub-areas.

Communication security includes encryption and network controls that require technical testing — MC-04 (approach selection) verifies the IESP did not rely solely on interviews for these technical areas.
Incident management assessment quality is verified through standard QA procedures across all 7 domains.

domainMappings.domainName: Information Security Aspects of Business Continuity Management
domainMappings.rationale: BCP/DR assessment involves risk-based scoping (SD-03) and requires appropriate approach selection (MC-04). Conclusion methodology (RQ-02) is critical for assessing resilience posture.
Online transaction security is customer-facing with high regulatory visibility — attestation accuracy (RQ-03) is particularly relevant as this Part often forms the basis for negative attestation.
Cloud governance is governed by Appendix 10 and requires assessors with cloud-specific competency — MC-02 (professional standards compliance) ensures the methodology addresses this specialised area.
Emerging technology (AI/ML) assessment requires specialised assessor competency (EA-02 verifies domain expertise) and risk-based scoping (SD-03) to identify applicable AI deployments.
Third-party risk assessment requires risk-based scoping (SD-03) to focus on critical service providers and appropriate assessment approaches (MC-04) for supply chain controls.

crossCuttingProcedures.description: These QA procedures apply across all IESP control domains — they assess the overall engagement quality rather than domain-specific content.

procedures.name: Engagement quality control
procedures.scope: Verifies IESP firm-level quality management applies to the entire engagement

procedures.name: NACSA/CE Directive methodology alignment
procedures.scope: Applies only when the assessed institution is also an NCII entity under the Cyber Security Act 2024

procedures.name: Workpaper quality and documentation standards
procedures.scope: Verifies workpaper quality across all domains assessed

procedures.name: Timeliness and completeness of fieldwork
procedures.scope: Verifies all planned procedures were completed within the engagement timeline

procedures.name: Conclusion methodology and basis
procedures.scope: Verifies the overall conclusion methodology is transparent and consistently applied

procedures.name: Negative attestation accuracy
procedures.scope: Verifies attestation language accuracy across the entire report

procedures.name: Timeliness of reporting and BNM submission
procedures.scope: Verifies report delivery and regulatory submission timelines

procedures.name: Board pack completeness
procedures.scope: Verifies the board deliberation pack covers all assessment Parts

procedures.name: Presentation quality and accessibility
procedures.scope: Verifies board presentation translates technical findings into business-impact terms

procedures.name: Action plan tracking and accountability
procedures.scope: Verifies management action plan has owners, timelines, and board approval

procedures.name: MAP tracking mechanism
procedures.scope: Verifies a formal mechanism exists for tracking remediation across all domains

procedures.name: Management action verification
procedures.scope: Verifies closed actions were independently validated, not self-certified

procedures.name: Recurring and unresolved issues
procedures.scope: Identifies findings that persist across assessment cycles and verifies escalation

procedures.name: BNM reporting of remediation progress
procedures.scope: Verifies regulatory reporting of remediation status where required by BNM
